title: Hello World — Building in Public
summary: Why I built this blog, what I'll write about, and the engineering decisions behind this site.
content:

After years of accumulating drafts in Notion and threads in private Slack channels, I decided it was time to build a proper home for my writing."}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"why-another-blog",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor","aria-label":"Link to section",href:"#why-another-blog",children:"Why another blog?"})}),"
",t(l.p,{children:["Most developer blogs are either generated from a template in 10 minutes or over-engineered to the point where the author never actually writes anything. I wanted something in between — a place that feels ",n(l.em,{children:"crafted"}),", but never gets in the way of the writing."]}),"
",n(i,{type:"info",children:t(l.p,{children:["This blog is fully open source. Press ",n(l.strong,{children:"⌘ K"})," to open the command palette and search anything on the site."]})}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"what-ill-write-about",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor","aria-label":"Link to section",href:"#what-ill-write-about",children:"What I’ll write about"})}),"
",t(l.ul,{children:["
",t(l.li,{children:[n(l.strong,{children:"Systems design"})," — scalability decisions, distributed systems trade-offs, architecture reviews."]}),"
",t(l.li,{children:[n(l.strong,{children:"Frontend engineering"})," — React, Next.js, performance, and the craft of building UIs."]}),"
",t(l.li,{children:[n(l.strong,{children:"Engineering culture"})," — team structures, code reviews, on-call, and the invisible labour of software."]}),"
",t(l.li,{children:[n(l.strong,{children:"Tools I build"})," — small utilities, scripts, and experiments."]}),"
"]}),"
",n(l.h2,{id:"the-stack",children:n(l.a,{className:"anchor","aria-label":"Link to section",href:"#the-stack",children:"The stack"})}),"
",n(l.p,{children:"This site is built with:"}),"

",t(l.span,{className:"line",children:[n(l.span,{style:{color:"#73DACA"},children:"  output"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:":"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:' "'}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#9ECE6A"},children:"export"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:'"'}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:","})]}),"
",t(l.span,{className:"line",children:[n(l.span,{style:{color:"#73DACA"},children:"  images"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:":"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#9ABDF5"},children:" {"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#73DACA"},children:" unoptimized"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:":"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#FF9E64"},children:" true"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#9ABDF5"},children:" }"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:","})]}),"
",t(l.span,{className:"line",children:[n(l.span,{style:{color:"#9ABDF5"},children:"}"}),n(l.span,{style:{color:"#89DDFF"},children:";"})]})]})})}),"
",n(l.p,{children:"Everything is statically generated at build time. No database, no server, no cold starts. Just HTML on a CDN."}),"
",n(l.hr,{}),"
",n(l.p,{children:"Thanks for reading. More soon."})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:t}=e.components||{};return t?n(t,{...e,children:n(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};

title: Why I Rewrote Our Build System (And What I Learned)
summary: A deep-dive into migrating a 300k-line monorepo from a bespoke Makefile + Bash pipeline to Turborepo, the trade-offs we made, and the lessons that surprised us.
content:

It worked — in the same way a 1998 Honda Civic with 300,000 miles “works”. You could get from A to B, but every journey carried existential dread."}),"
",n(i.h2,{id:"the-legacy-system",children:n(i.a,{className:"anchor","aria-label":"Link to section",href:"#the-legacy-system",children:"The legacy system"})}),"
",n(i.p,{children:"Here’s what we were dealing with:"}),"
",n(a,{children:".
├── Makefile          # 2,400 lines, 0 comments
├── scripts/
│   ├── build.sh      # Called from Make, calls Python, calls Node
│   ├── test.sh       # 14 environment variables, 3 undocumented
│   └── deploy.sh     # Everyone is afraid of this file
└── ci/
  └── pipeline.yml  # 8 minutes on a warm cache, 23 minutes cold"}),"
",n(s,{type:"warn",children:r(i.p,{children:["Before migrating any build system in production, make sure you have at least ",n(i.strong,{children:"one week"})," of baseline metrics: build times, cache hit rates, failure rates. You cannot prove improvement without a baseline."]})}),"
",n(i.h2,{id:"why-turborepo",children:n(i.a,{className:"anchor","aria-label":"Link to section",href:"#why-turborepo",children:"Why Turborepo"})}),"
",n(i.p,{children:"I evaluated three options:"}),"
",r(i.table,{children:[n(i.thead,{children:r(i.tr,{children:[n(i.th,{children:"Tool"}),n(i.th,{children:"Remote cache"}),n(i.th,{children:"Incremental"}),n(i.th,{children:"Learning curve"})]})}),r(i.tbody,{children:[r(i.tr,{children:[n(i.td,{children:"Turborepo"}),n(i.td,{children:"✅ native"}),n(i.td,{children:"✅ task-level"}),n(i.td,{children:"Low"})]}),r(i.tr,{children:[n(i.td,{children:"Nx"}),n(i.td,{children:"✅"}),n(i.td,{children:"✅ project-level"}),n(i.td,{children:"Medium"})]}),r(i.tr,{children:[n(i.td,{children:"Bazel"}),n(i.td,{children:"✅"}),n(i.td,{children:"✅ file-level"}),n(i.td,{children:"Extreme"})]})]})]}),"

",r(i.p,{children:["The biggest surprise was not the time savings. It was the ",n(i.strong,{children:"psychological effect"})," on the team. When builds are fast, engineers run them more often. When engineers run them more often, they catch bugs earlier. The feedback loop compounds."]}),"
